Registry / Terms / specified measuring ranges

specified measuring ranges

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #02074 · 2 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 2 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

2 distinct definitions across 2 publications.

2 unique definitions (each used by only one publication)

Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Definition
20102007OIML R 140:2007T.2.802074
set of values of measurands or quantities characteristic of the gas 1. This definition applies to the measuring for which the error is intended to lie within the limits specified s in this Recommendation. In general the upper and lower limits t of the specified measuring range are called maximum value and 2. Main minimum value, respectively (for example: maximum flowrate 2 characte 000 m3/h, minimum flowrate 50 m3/h) f 3 m p
pending
202X2007OIML R 140:2007T.2.802074
set of values of measurands or quantities characteristic of the gas for which the error is intended to lie within the limits specified in this Recommendation. In general the upper and lower limits of the specified measuring range are called maximum value and minimum value, respectively (for example: maximum flowrate 2000 m3/h, minimum flowrate 50 m3/h)
pending
